person.name: Sir Knight Robert Hoo, Lord of Luton II

nameForms.fullText: Sir Knight Robert Hoo, Lord of Luton II

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Sir knight Robert HOO II (1114-1166) Lord of Luton & Rosamond CHIVERON (1146-1191)

Robert was born in Litcham, Mitford, County Norfolk, England and died on August 1, 1166 in Ampthill, County Bedfordshire, England. Rosamond was born in 1146 and died on July 23, 1191 in Luton Hoo, County Bedfordshire, England.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Knight Robert Hoo, Lord of Luton II於1114年出生在英國諾福爾可米特福德時，他的父親Sir Robert Hoo Knight37歲，母親Milicina Princess of Malmaynes30歲。他於1163年與Rosamond de Chiveron在英國貝德福爾盧頓 胡結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1166年8月1日在英國英格蘭貝德福爾盧頓 胡享年52歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭貝德福爾莫爾登。

nameMeanings.name: Hoo,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Wai Lam, Wing, Beng, Chin, Gan, Hing, Hui, Kee, Lai, Lai Ching, Mei-Mei.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surname   Hu  .

variants.name: Hu
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Ming, Ping, Bin, Yi, Ying, Jing, Xiao, Qi, Yan, Chi.  Korean Chang, Chung, Min, Yiping, Chong, Chul, Hu, Jung Soo, Sang Hoon, Tian, Wook, Yiming.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 胡, a term referring to the ethnic minorities in ancient northern and western China: (i) from Hu (胡), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Henan province) during the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771 BC ). After it was annexed by the state of Zheng (located in present-day Shaanxi and Henan provinces) in the 26th year of the reign of King Ping of Zhou (died 720 BC ), people from the fief adopted 胡, the name of the state, as their surname. (ii) from Hu (胡), the name of a state (located in present-day Anhui province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu in the 15th year of the reign of Duke Ding of Lu (ruler of the state of Lu, reigned 509–495 BC ), people from the fief adopted 胡, the name of the state, as their surname. (iii) from the personal name of Hu Gong Man (胡公满, Duke Hu of Chen), the posthumous title of Yu Man. He was given the fief of Chen (located mainly in present-day Henan and Anhui province) by King Wu of Zhou (c. 1087–1043 BC ). (iv) from the personal name of Hu Guang (胡廣), who changed his original surname 黃 to 胡 during the Eastern Han dynasty (25–220 AD ). (v) from the Hu Gu (胡骨) or He Gu (纥骨) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their surname to 胡 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 扈, meaning ‘retinue’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Hu (扈), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Shaanxi province) dating back to the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ) (ii) adopted as a surname by the Hu Di Gan (扈地干) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

variants.name: Hooe
meanings: English: variant of   Hoo   1.

nameMeanings.name: Lord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English laford, louerd, lord ‘lord, master’ (Old English hlāford), perhaps given to a someone who played the part of the lord or master of ceremonies in festival games.
Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as a translation of the main element of Gaelic Ó Tighearnaigh (see Tierney ) and Mac Thighearnáin (see McKiernan ).
Americanized form of French Laure, a variant of the personal name Laur 3.

variants.name: Tierney
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, John Patrick, Brigid, Clodagh, Kieran, Michael Sean, Siobhan.

Irish: shortened form of O'Tierney, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Tíghearnaigh ‘descendant of Tighearnach’, a byname meaning ‘lord, master’. The Gaelic name is sometimes ‘translated’ as English Lord . Compare Tiernan .

variants.name: Lords
meanings: English: variant of Lord , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: Luton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name, chiefly from Litton in Somerset, and occasionally from Luton in Bishopsteignton, Devon.

variants.name: Lutton
meanings: habitational name from East and West Lutton in Weaverthorpe (East Yorkshire), or from Lutton in Sutton Saint Nicholas (Lincolnshire).
variant of   Luton  .
English:

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Knight Robert Hoo, Lord of Luton II was born in 1114, in Mitford, Norfolk, England, his father, Sir Robert Hoo Knight, was 37 and his mother, Milicina Princess of Malmaynes, was 30. He married Rosamond de Chiveron in 1163, in Luton Hoo, Bedfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died on 1 August 1166, in Luton Hoo, Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 52, and was buried in Maulden, Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Knight Robert Hoo, Lord of Luton II est né en 1114, à Mitford, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Sir Robert Hoo Knight, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Milicina Princess of Malmaynes, avait 30 ans. Il a épousé Rosamond de Chiveron en 1163, à Luton Hoo, Bedfordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé le 1 août 1166, à Luton Hoo, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 52 ans, et a été enterré à Maulden, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Knight Robert Hoo, Lord of Luton II è nato il nel 1114 nel Mitford, Norfolk, England, il suo padre, Sir Robert Hoo Knight, aveva 37 anni e il suo madre, Milicina Princess of Malmaynes, aveva 30 anni. Lui sposato Rosamond de Chiveron nel 1163, nel Luton Hoo, Bedfordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. ja.lifeSummary: Sir Knight Robert Hoo, Lord of Luton IIが1114年にイギリスノーフォルクミトフォードで生まれた時，父Sir Robert Hoo Knightは37歳，母Milicina Princess of Malmaynesは30歳であった。1163年にイギリスベドフォードシュアイアルトン ホーオでRosamond de Chiveronと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼は1166年8月1日にイギリスイギリスベドフォードシュアイアルトン ホーオで52歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスベドフォードシュアイアマウルデンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Knight Robert Hoo, Lord of Luton II(이)가 1114년 영국 노르폴크 미트퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Robert Hoo Knight(은)는 37세였고, 그의 어머니 Milicina Princess of Malmaynes(은)는 30세였다. 그는 1163년 영국 베드퍼드실르 루턴 후에서 Rosamond de Chiveron(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1166년 8월 1일 영국 잉글랜드 베드퍼드실르 루턴 후에서 52세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 베드퍼드실르 몰든에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Knight Robert Hoo, Lord of Luton II nasceu em 1114, em Mitford, Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Robert Hoo Knight, tinha 37 anos e sua mãe, Milicina Princess of Malmaynes, tinha 30 anos. Ele casou-se com Rosamond de Chiveron em 1163, em Luton Hoo, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1 de agosto de 1166, em Luton Hoo, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 52 anos, e foi sepultado em Maulden, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Knight Robert Hoo, Lord of Luton II родился в 1114 г. в местности митфорд, норфолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Robert Hoo Knight было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Milicina Princess of Malmaynes, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Rosamond de Chiveron в 1163 г. в местности лутон ху, бедфордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер 1 августа 1166 г. в местности лутон ху, бедфордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 52 лет и был похоронен в местности маулден, бедфордшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.Ampthill, County Bedfordshire, England: Luton Hoo, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Knight Robert Hoo, Lord of Luton II nació en 1114, en Mitford, Norfolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Robert Hoo Knight, tenía 37 años y su mamá, Milicina Princess of Malmaynes, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Rosamond de Chiveron en 1163, en Luton Hoo, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió el 1 de agosto de 1166, en Luton Hoo, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 52 años, y fue sepultado en Maulden, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
